text: Hugin is a worker/pipeline, not a peer service. Should be framed as ETL with idempotency, not an independent app.
Mímir /search endpoint violates the 'Munin is the single query interface' principle stated in the plan.
Tag syntax (client:lofalk) is incompatible with Munin's current regex validation /^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9_-]*$/.
rsync --delete propagates accidental deletions. No manifest, checksums, or freshness contract.
Artifact indexing sends private customer data through Claude API, contradicting sovereignty principle.
No integrity model (checksums) for referenced files. Munin summary can silently describe wrong version.
Core product promise depends on unproven multi-hop authenticated fetch by remote agents.
Mímir drifting from 'thin gateway' to 'second application' with growing feature surface.
No monitoring or alerting story for three services across two Pis with cron jobs.
Prompt-injection and hostile content handling not addressed for an AI memory system ingesting external content.
Bypassing Cloudflare Access for Mímir is risky — leaked bearer token gives full archive access to customer documents.
Build order should start with proving remote fetch path and resolving tag/content-size/identity before bulk work.
Hugin depends on source definitions, not full people profiles. Dependency stated too broadly.
X API cost ($100/mo) and existing x-mcp-server instability make X integration unrealistic for MVP.
Path-based key + SHA-256 is not a real identity model. Renames and edits create ambiguity.
'Implicit consent' for on-demand cloud summarization of private docs is a mushy policy boundary.
Content-size strategy for private docs is internally inconsistent: no AI summary + truncated text + possible chunking — pick one.
Plan has two incompatible success criteria: 'any platform transparent deep access' vs 'works even without remote fetch.' Must pick one.
Cloudflare Access edge policy for Mímir still unresolved despite being flagged in Round 1.
Prompt-injection handling still entirely absent from response despite being flagged in Round 1.
